commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; maintainers should make the product judgment on the new doctor warning and proceed through normal CI/merge gates.; Cleared: The diff only touches TypeScript catalog/doctor diagnostics, focused tests, and changelog text, with no dependency, workflow, secret-handling, or privileged execution changes. (sha=bb8c98989ed5c49ff8d078f687e94e6e233150e2)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `catalogSource` provenance for provider-supplemental model catalog rows and a warn-only `openclaw doctor` contribution for explicit `agents.defaults.models` allowlists that hide those rows.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main appends provider-supplemental rows without marker provenance and only warns about the narrower `hooks.gmail.model` allowlist case. I did not run a local doctor command because this review is read-only, but the PR body now supplies after-fix terminal output from the real command.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; maintainers should make the product judgment on the new doctor warning and proceed through normal CI/merge gates.

target.title: feat(doctor): warn about hidden provider catalog models
target.body:
## Summary
- mark provider-supplied supplemental model catalog rows with stable catalog provenance
- add a warn-only doctor contribution for explicit agents.defaults.models allowlists hiding provider-supplied catalog models
- classify the new doctor contribution in the current doctor health conversion plan
- keep opt-in guidance warn-only; no allowlist is changed automatically

Fixes #39992.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor` did not warn when an explicit `agents.defaults.models` allowlist hid provider-supplied supplemental catalog rows, so newly exposed provider models could be unavailable without operator guidance.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `bb8c98989e`, using the repo test runner against the real doctor contribution and model catalog modules.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-77432-72sxpu NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive`, `pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts`, and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from a local OpenClaw source checkout using a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` with an explicit allowlist (only `openai/gpt-5.4`) and provider catalog rows available:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-77432-72sxpu NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive
◇  Models
│  32 provider-supplied catalog models are available but not in
│  agents.defaults.models.
│  Agents with an explicit allowlist cannot select these models until you
│  opt in.
│  - byteplus/glm-4-7-251222
│  - byteplus/kimi-k2-5-260127
│  - byteplus/seed-1-8-251228
│  - byteplus-plan/ark-code-latest
│  - byteplus-plan/doubao-seed-code
│  - ... 22 more
│  To add one: openclaw config set agents.defaults.models
│  '{"byteplus/glm-4-7-251222":{}}' --strict-json --merge
│  Review provider/cost boundaries before adding new provider-supplied
│  models.
Config: /tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-77432-72sxpu/.openclaw/openclaw.json
└  Doctor complete.

$ pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 30.10s
unit-fast: 1 file passed, 22 tests passed
unit: 1 file passed, 3 tests passed
agents: 1 file passed, 29 tests passed

$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: The real `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` path now emits the user-visible `Models` warning when an explicit `agents.defaults.models` allowlist hides provider-supplied catalog rows, including concrete hidden model examples and an opt-in `openclaw config set agents.defaults.models ... --strict-json --merge` command. The focused repo tests still pass for the contribution, catalog merge, and conversion-plan coverage.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps in the targeted doctor warning path; full repository CI covers build and regression breadth.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this follow-up, CI failed `checks-node-core-src-security` in `src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts` because `doctor:model-allowlist-catalog` was present in contributions but missing from `doctorHealthConversionRules`.

## Testing
- pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts
- git diff --check
target.author: sercada

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
